channel.title: Reading - Category - 滑翔闪'S BLOG
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: The Berglas Effect
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: The moment you witness a miracle

item.title: A Copernican Moment and Research Taste
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: That moment then, and this moment now, are more alike than they look.

item.title: Shanxi Datong Engagement Case Second Trial: An Economic Perspective on Judicial Spirit and Execution
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: An economic perspective on the second trial of the Shanxi Datong engagement case, focusing on judicial spirit, procedural justice, and information asymmetry.

item.title: The modernity of detective fiction
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: This post draws on Tuili Da Wuxian to think about the narrative appeal of detective fiction and, above all, its relationship to modernity.

item.title: The Origin of Rich Species: A Lamp Can Light a Room Dark for a Thousand Years
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: The Origin of Rich Species challenges the mainstream academic reading of the Malthusian trap and builds a Darwinian view of economic history.

item.title: The Mathematical History of Economics
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Reading notes on Shi Shuzhong’s book about the development of mathematical tools in economics.

item.title: The Rhetoric of Economics
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: This article introduces an unusual perspective on economic writing by examining economic arguments and mathematical models through the lens of literary criticism.

item.title: 《Crossing Boundaries of Community》: Fluid Relations and the Origins of Communities
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Xiang Biao’s 《Crossing Boundaries of Community》 is straightforward and humorous, exploring his research methodology and focusing on new community development models.

item.title: Pursuing Scholarship: Beyond 'Serving the World' and 'Because the Mountain Is There'
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: This article explores the essence of academic pursuit through Wang Xiaobo’s works and the spirit of science, emphasizing that the drive for answers stems from curiosity and desire for knowledge, rather than pursuing utility or practicality.

item.title: Goodbye, Eri: Not Remembering Others, but Knowing Oneself
item.author: 滑翔闪
item.description: Behind every act of madness lies a resilient and pure heart. The key to understanding ‘Goodbye, Eri’ is not about remembering others but realizing Yuta was filming himself all along. Words fail to convey how much effort I’ve poured into loving this world.
